properties.remark: CORRAL WALLS DOWN. WINDOWS ON SOUTH SIDE OF OLD BUILDING WERE BLOWN OUT. STEEL CHAIRS ON SOUTH SIDE OF DECK BLOWN OFF DECK. TREE BRANCHES DOWN. SOME SHINGLES IN YARDS. POWER OUTAGES IN THE AREA.

TREE FELL ON POWER LINE...CAUSING THE POWER LINE TO FALL DOWN.
LEANING POWER POLES AND LARGE TREE BRANCHES DOWN ALONG COUNTY ROAD 6 BETWEEN EPPING AND SPRINGBROOK.
MANY SMALL TREE LIMBS DOWN...BUT A FEW LARGER LIMBS OF 4 INCH DIAMETER ALSO DOWN. A ROUND METAL PICNIC TABLE TURNED OVER AS WELL. SOME PEA SIZED HAIL THAT BEGAN AROUND 605 PM CDT. HEAVY RAIN AS WELL.
SPOTTER ESTIMATES 50 TO 60 MPH GUSTS LASTED FOR 15 MINUTES.

WOODEN FRAMED STRUCTURE COVERED IN SHEET METAL WITH AN OPEN BAY FACING EAST WAS COMPLETELY BLOWN OVER AND APART. DEBRIS SEPARATE FROM THE BUILDING WAS ALL FOUND 30 YARDS TO THE WEST OF THE STRUCTURE. WINDS WERE REPORTED TO BE FROM EAST TO WEST...BLOWING INTO THE OPEN BAY. WOODEN SUPPORT POSTS THREE FEET INTO THE GROUND WERE BROKEN AT GROUND LEVEL...WITH ONE POST COMPLETELY OUT OF THE GROUND. REPORT OBTAINED THROUGH THE MEDIA.
COVERED GROUND
LASTED ABOUT 8 MINUTES. GROUND IS COVERED
TORNADO ON GROUND. SIGHT OF TORNADO WAS LOST IN RAIN SHAFT 3 MINUTES AFTER BEING SPOTTED.

2 VOLUNTEER FIRE FIGHTERS WERE DRIVING EAST AWAY FROM A TORNADO. THE BACK WINDOWS OF BOTH CARS WERE KNOCKED OUT BY WIND AND BLOWN INTO THE CAB AND THE CARS WERE BLOWN INTO A DITCH. THE TORNADO WAS SEEN FOR AT LEAST 15 MINUTES.
TORNADO ON THE GROUND FOR A FEW MINUTES THEN LIFTED BUT FUNNEL CLOUD IS STILL PRESENT.
TORNADO
OLD OAK BARN DESTROYED. DEBRIS FIELD SPANS 200 TO 300 FEET TO THE SOUTH. TIME OF DAMAGE IS ESTIMATED.
LARGE TREES 12 INCH DIAMETER UPROOTED. SURVEYED THE MORNING OF JULY 9. TIME OF DAMAGE IS ESTIMATED.
FROM 2 TO 5 MILES EAST OF PORTAL THERE WERE MANY LARGE TREES - SOME 12 INCH DIAMETER - DOWN AND A GRANARY WAS DESTROYED. TIME OF DAMAGE IS ESTIMATED.

EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT HAS RECEIVED EYE WITNESS REPORTS OF A TORNADO WITHIN THE DICKINSON CITY LIMITS WEDNESDAY EVENING. THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE WILL DO A STORM DAMAGE SURVEY OF THE AREA THIS AFTERNOON AND DETERMINE TORNADO INTENSITY. THE WORST DAMAGE IS REPORTED TO BE IN THE SOUTHWEST PART OF THE CITY.
60 TO 70 FOOT EVERGREEN TREES DOWN IN SOUTH DICKINSON. REPRORT RELAYED BY THE MEDIA.

ABOUT 2.50 INCHES OF RAIN. FLASH FLOODING ALONG 8 MILE CREEK. THE CREEK WAS OUT OF ITS BANKS AND FLOWING INTO PASTURES. FENCE POSTS WERE WASHED OUT AND SOME ROADS HAD MUD AND SILT DEEP ENOUGH THAT THEY COULD NOT BE CROSSED. LATE REPORT.

ESTIMATED 50 TO 60 MPH WINDS LASTED 5 MINUTES. PEA SIZED HAIL AND FREQUENT LIGHTNING ALSO OCCURRED.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE RECEIVED SEVERAL REPORTS OF THIS WATERSPOUT AND OTHER FUNNEL CLOUDS OVER LAKE SAKAKAWEA THIS MORNING. A REPORTER WITH MHA TIMES OF NEW TOWN FORWARDED A PICTURE HE TOOK OF THE WATERSPOUT. IN THE PICTURE IT IS EVIDENT THAT THE SPOUT IS OVER THE LAKE...IS IN CONTACT WITH THE WATER...AND THAT WATER IS BEING LIFTED INTO THE AIR. THE SPOUT DISSIPATED BEFORE REACHING LAND. IT WAS VISIBLE FOR SEVERAL MINUTES.
